name: n8n-mcp-tools-expert
description: Expert guide for using n8n-mcp MCP tools effectively. Use when searching for nodes, validating configurations, accessing templates, managing workflows, or using any n8n-mcp tool. Provides tool selection guidance, parameter formats, and common patterns.
capabilities: Search and discover n8n nodes by keyword using search_nodes tool
Get detailed node information including operations, properties, and examples
Validate node configurations using different validation profiles
Create, update, and manage n8n workflows via API
Search and deploy from a library of 2700+ workflow templates
Access comprehensive documentation for all MCP tools
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 170 potential issues including external_commands (96), network (1), and env_access (1). After evaluating the actual content, all findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The backtick characters detected are markdown code formatting for tool names, not shell execution. The URL is a legitimate source reference. The API key reference is documentation about required configuration. This is a documentation file with example code snippets, not executable malicious code.
valueStatement: This skill provides expert guidance for using n8n-mcp MCP server tools to discover nodes, validate configurations, and manage workflows efficiently.
userTitle: Build n8n Workflows with MCP Tools
limitations: Requires n8n instance running with API access for workflow management tools
Some advanced tools require specific n8n versions
Template deployment requires credentials to be set up in target n8n instance
Does not provide visual workflow editing - only API-based management

useCases.targetUser: Developers new to n8n who need guidance on node selection and configuration
useCases.title: Find and configure n8n nodes
useCases.description: Search for nodes by keyword, get detailed configuration options, and validate settings before building workflows.

useCases.targetUser: AI developers building workflow automation with Claude or Codex
useCases.title: Build workflows iteratively
useCases.description: Create workflows step-by-step using n8n_update_partial_workflow with intelligent error handling and validation.

useCases.targetUser: Automation engineers who want quick starts for common workflow patterns
useCases.title: Deploy from template library
useCases.description: Search 2700+ pre-built workflows, get details, and deploy directly to your n8n instance with auto-fix capabilities.

promptTemplates.title: Find a node for specific task
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to [describe your task, e.g., send messages to Slack]. Use the n8n-mcp-tools skill to search for appropriate n8n nodes and show me how to configure the main settings.
promptTemplates.scenario: Discovering the right node for a specific automation task

promptTemplates.title: Validate node configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me validate my n8n node configuration. I'm setting up a [node name] node with these settings: [describe config]. Use validate_node with the runtime profile to check for errors.
promptTemplates.scenario: Checking configuration before deploying a workflow

promptTemplates.title: Create a new workflow
promptTemplates.prompt: I want to create an n8n workflow that [describe workflow purpose]. Use n8n-mcp-tools to create the workflow with appropriate nodes and show me the structure.
promptTemplates.scenario: Building a new workflow from scratch

promptTemplates.title: Find and deploy a template
promptTemplates.prompt: Search the n8n template library for workflows related to [describe use case, e.g., webhook processing]. Show me the top results and how to deploy one to my n8n instance.
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick-starting a project from existing templates

bestPractices: Use get_node with detail: 'standard' (default) for most use cases - it covers 95% of needs while keeping token usage low
Specify validation profile explicitly - 'runtime' profile is recommended for pre-deployment checks
Use smart parameters like 'branch' and 'case' for IF and Switch nodes instead of manual sourceIndex calculations
antiPatterns: Using detail: 'full' by default - this returns 3-8K tokens and should only be used for debugging complex configurations
Forgetting the nodeType prefix - use 'nodes-base.*' for search/validate tools and 'n8n-nodes-base.*' for workflow tools
Trying to build entire workflows in one shot - use iterative updates with validation between each change

faq.question: What is the difference between nodes-base and n8n-nodes-base prefixes?
faq.answer: Use 'nodes-base.slack' for search, get_node, and validate_node tools. Use 'n8n-nodes-base.slack' for workflow creation and update tools. Using the wrong prefix will cause 'Node not found' errors.

faq.question: Do I need an n8n API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. Node discovery, validation, and template search tools work without API access. Only workflow management tools (create, update, deploy) require N8N_API_URL and N8N_API_KEY.

faq.question: How do I choose the right validation profile?
faq.answer: Use 'minimal' for quick checks, 'runtime' for pre-deployment (recommended), 'ai-friendly' for AI-generated configs, and 'strict' for production validation.

faq.question: What is auto-sanitization in n8n workflow updates?
faq.answer: When you update a workflow, n8n automatically fixes certain issues like binary operator structures and missing IF/Switch metadata. It cannot fix broken connections or paradoxical states.

faq.question: Can I use this skill to edit existing workflows?
faq.answer: Yes, use n8n_update_partial_workflow with operations like addNode, addConnection, or removeNode. The skill supports 17 different operation types.

faq.question: How do smart parameters work for node connections?
faq.answer: Instead of manually calculating sourceIndex (0, 1, 2...), use semantic parameters like 'branch: true/false' for IF nodes or 'case: 0, 1, 2...' for Switch nodes.
